paper_title: Field Sampling and SPI Analysis in the Bay of Tunis

methods.description: Monthly sampling and analysis of sea surface parameters and phytoplankton in the Bay of Tunis to evaluate nutrient inputs and ecological dynamics.

steps.description: Monthly sampling was carried out (December 1993 to November 1995) at nine stations located in the central waters of the Bay of Tunis.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct monthly sampling at specified stations in the Bay of Tunis

steps.description: Eight additional stations were included along the coastline in order to evaluate nutrient inputs of terrestrial origin.
steps.searchable_summary: include additional coastal stations for sampling

steps.description: At each station, sea surface temperature, salinity, ammonium, nitrate, nitrite, phosphate, silicate and chlorophyll a were measured.
steps.searchable_summary: measure sea surface parameters at each station

steps.description: Analyses of nutrients were carried out using a spectrophotometric method (model Beckman 36 UV-vis) according to FAO (1975) for nitrite, nitrate, phosphate and silicate, and according to AMINOT & CHAUSSEPIED (1983) for ammonium.

For chlorophyll a analysis, water samples were filtered through Whatman GF/C filters, frozen and measured with a Spectrophotometer (model Beckman 36 UV-vis.) according to the Lorenzen method described in UNESCO (1966).

Surface water samples for phytoplankton analysis were fixed with Lugol's iodine solution and sub-samples of 25 ml were analysed under a Leitz inverted microscope following a sedimentation time of 24 h.
Cells were counted along two diametric transects of the bottom plate for dominant species, and by sweeping the whole surface for rare species (THRONDSEN, 1995).
Meteorological data (air temperature and precipitation) were monitored daily at the Tunis Airport meteorological station 10 Km from the sampling stations at 4 m above sea level.

methods.description: Application of the Specific Preference Index (SPI) to analyze the spatio-temporal distribution of dominant diatom and dinoflagellate species in relation to environmental parameters.

steps.description: The data-base was performed in order to collect enough information related to the phytoplankton dynamics.
In order to resolve the difficulties we used a new index called Specific Preference Index (SPI).
steps.searchable_summary: apply SPI to analyze species distribution

steps.description: This index applied to dominant diatoms and dinoflagellates pointed out clearly the seasonal patterns of these species.
The SPI identifies the apparent ecological niches of the selected species characterized by temperature, salinity and nutrients.
